title: A new CRASH clock measures the chance of satellite collisions, and it's ticking down fast - Phys.org
description: A new CRASH clock measures the chance of satellite collisions, and it's ticking down fast  Phys.orgA new CRASH Clock measures the chance of satellite collisions, and it’s ticking down fast  The ConversationIn 2018, low Earth orbit still had months of margin: if satellites stopped dodging one another, researchers estimate it could take 164 days before a catastrophic collision. By 2025, after the rise of megaconstellations, that margin had collapsed to just 5.5 days.  Space Daily
